propositions: Builds ground truth by construction: takes real cleanly-closed plans and seeds exactly one defect per class across five defect classes (absent asserted capability, layer-cake split, consumed-artifact-without-ordering-edge, unnecessary mechanism, untagged operational AC), with the mutation itself as the label.

Runs the current shipped plan-review gate over each mutant and its unmutated control to record a per-class baseline detection/FP table; MISS classes (1,4,5) are expected low/near-zero, and class 5 has no current detector by design.

The cleanly-closed filter requires post_claim_edit_class==none, reopen_count==0, force_close==False, completion_verifier_fail_count==0, had_persisted_review==True, live closed status, and ≥2 children for container classes 2 and 3.

Pre-registers per-class detection targets and blocking-FP bounds plus an INDETERMINATE run-validity cap in e2_thresholds.json, committed before the baseline run, serving as targets for later R1/R2/R3/R4 candidate detectors rather than E2's own pass condition.

Counting rules treat INDETERMINATE verdicts as not-detected on mutants and non-FP on controls, count advisory findings as detection, and count only block findings as blocking-FP; a run-validity guard refuses to emit the baseline when any class exceeds a 0.20 indeterminate fraction.

The gate is executed via a new mechanism that redirects ticket reads to a tmp mutant-store clone (REBAR_TRACKER_DIR + REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=off) while grounding code against the real repo, leaving the shared .tickets-tracker HEAD unchanged.
